name: seren-corpuscallosum
description: Gives GitHub Copilot one federated search across the whole Seren memory brain - SerenCorpusCallosum fans your query across every configured store (keyed facts, episodic memory, and more) and returns one rank-fused list.

serenCorpusCallosum.endpoint.description: Base URL of the SerenCorpusCallosum service. Default port 7423 matches SerenCorpusCallosum's own default.

serenCorpusCallosum.startCommand.description: Shell command used to start the SerenCorpusCallosum service when it is not reachable (the installed console script; or use 'python -m seren_corpus_callosum'). Application-scoped so workspace settings cannot override it (security: prevents a malicious .vscode/settings.json from injecting a different command).

serenCorpusCallosum.suppressStartPrompt.description: Suppress the 'service not reachable - start it?' prompt on extension activation. Use this if you intentionally run SerenCorpusCallosum only for some projects.

serenCorpusCallosum.bearerTokenHint.markdownDescription:
**Bearer token** is stored securely in the OS keychain, not here.

To set or update it, run the command:

`Seren CorpusCallosum: Set Bearer Token` (via `Ctrl+Shift+P`)

languageModelTools.name: seren_corpuscallosum_search
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search across every Seren memory store at once (keyed facts + episodic memory + more), rank-fused into one list.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search the entire Seren memory brain in one call. SerenCorpusCallosum is the *fan*: it sends your query to every configured store at once - keyed facts (SerenLoci), episodic/consolidated memory (SerenMemory), and any other stores wired in - and merges the results into a single ranked list using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (it reads each store's rank ORDER, never raw scores, so the merge stays correct even when stores use different embedders). Each hit carries its origin `store`, its `store_rank` within that store, the cross-store `score`, and `base_relevance`; `stores_searched` and `skipped` tell you which stores actually answered (a slow/down store is skipped, never fatal). Call this BEFORE answering anything that might lean on a recorded project convention, a hard-won fact, or past context - when you want the best recall across EVERYTHING and don't care which part of the brain it lives in. Reach for store-specific tools (e.g. search_loci for facts, recall for episodic memory) only when you already know which hemisphere you want.

query.description: Natural-language search query - the associative jump ('that CUDA thing on the Xavier', 'our brace rule').

n_results.description: Max merged hits to return after rank-fusion.
